comment.body:
You using that bounce after the 27th of January correction helps my point even more. That bounce is similar to the 275+ point bounce we just saw. The market then went much lower after that. However, after going lower, it did a massive bear market bounce… the type of bounce I was referring to would need to occur at 5300’s to match the decline preceding it.

So what I’m saying is, 2-3 days could make a 150-250 point difference in your long entry for this type of bounce. That was my point is all.

comment.body:
Yeah I see your point. Just trying to demonstrate that the bounce wasn’t much at all. It took 14 days the bounce. Didn’t even reach 5%. It failed to recover half the drop which took 20 days (tied 5th fastest -10% correction since 1950). 

I don’t think any of the narratives are correct of crash, bear market, crisis, and whatever else is out there. Definitely a shift. But I think that will just bring a whole lot of wide range sideways based on the past 2 months

comment.body: Thanks Alma and I learned a lot Since I joined this SUB. I want to say thanks  Alma and everyone  who contributed in this SUB really appreciated. For me, I think the very important thing I learn is "one should distinguish between 'TREND' and 'RALLY", just as Alma said today. Sometimes do you have a feeling that If you follow the TREND (which is downward as Alma post in daily and week post) , even though you make some mistakes in the trading, you still  make money or at least you will not lose much. However, If you are fighting the trend, lets's say , you want to capture the SPY or QQQ rally today, it would be very dangeous. IT does not mean that one should not capture the RALLY or Mean reversion trade in the downward, but when you do that, you should be really careful or quick in quick out, because in the big pictures, you are fighting the trend. That is my basic understanding learning from this SUB and why I still make money today. Thanks

comment.body: What a week. Last week everyone was on a high. We had a great week last week. This week (the week post OPEX) usually is week and it turned out to be so. I’d say for myself I was fighting with my own cognitive dissonance on several trades - i went against the levels that were downside breakdown levels. Too much hubris to exit the trades. Today I did exit when I needed to. Not so in other other cases. Hope is not a trading plan.
